scenario_complexity_basis: General object/person localization with limited crowd and temporal complexity
evaluation_protocol_basis: Standard detection-style evaluation; no identity or trajectory protocol
deployment_readiness_basis: Generic image data; limited deployment-specific pedestrian diversity
main_limitation: Not designed for dense pedestrian detection, temporal identity continuity, or tracking evaluation
pspr_interpretation: Detection-support resource for pretraining/benchmarking generic person localization, not a full MPT benchmark.

scenario_complexity_basis: Object-scale variation, clutter, overlapping objects, and diverse everyday scenes
evaluation_protocol_basis: Standardized detection/segmentation metrics; no identity or association evaluation
deployment_readiness_basis: Large and diverse image corpus, but not tracking-specific
main_limitation: Image-level dataset without identity continuity or trajectory annotations
pspr_interpretation: Useful for detector pretraining in tracking-by-detection pipelines, but cannot evaluate MPT association.

scenario_complexity_basis: Scale variation, partial occlusion, urban road scenes, and pedestrian localization
evaluation_protocol_basis: Pedestrian detection benchmark with established detection metrics and protocols
deployment_readiness_basis: Real urban driving videos with pedestrian detection relevance
main_limitation: Detection-oriented; does not evaluate identity preservation or trajectory association
pspr_interpretation: Supports pedestrian detector training/evaluation for MPT pipelines, not complete tracking evaluation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Small-scale pedestrians, occlusion, amodal/full-body localization, and urban clutter
evaluation_protocol_basis: Pedestrian detection metrics and splits; no identity-aware tracking protocol
deployment_readiness_basis: Urban street scenes with realistic pedestrian localization challenges
main_limitation: Limited value for evaluating association, temporal continuity, or identity recovery
pspr_interpretation: Strong detector-support dataset for urban pedestrian localization, but not a trajectory benchmark.

scenario_complexity_basis: Heavy crowding, body overlap, full/visible/head boxes, and partial visibility
evaluation_protocol_basis: Detection AP/miss-rate style evaluation; no temporal identity metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Dense pedestrian images but limited video-level deployment context
main_limitation: Does not evaluate temporal tracking or identity continuity
pspr_interpretation: Useful for crowded-scene detector pretraining before tracking on MOT20-like benchmarks.

scenario_complexity_basis: Dense pedestrian localization, strong scale variation, and high person density
evaluation_protocol_basis: Detection-oriented evaluation; no identity or trajectory protocol
deployment_readiness_basis: Crowded pedestrian scenes with useful detection variation
main_limitation: Image-level dataset without track-level identity information
pspr_interpretation: Useful for pedestrian detector robustness in dense scenes, not for evaluating MPT end-to-end.

scenario_complexity_basis: City-scale variation, scale changes, traffic-scene complexity, and adverse-condition relevance
evaluation_protocol_basis: Detection-style evaluation; no trajectory-level identity preservation metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Urban traffic scenes across multiple cities with deployment-relevant variability
main_limitation: Mainly supports detection and does not directly evaluate long-term identity association
pspr_interpretation: Useful for deployment-oriented pedestrian detector training in driving or urban MPT pipelines.

scenario_complexity_basis: Dense pedestrian localization, multi-camera calibration, and cross-view spatial consistency
evaluation_protocol_basis: Multi-camera localization/detection protocol; identity tracking protocol is limited
deployment_readiness_basis: Synchronized multi-view outdoor surveillance with calibrated cameras
main_limitation: Limited calibrated scene scale; not a broad temporal MOT benchmark
pspr_interpretation: Useful for multi-camera pedestrian localization and cross-view detection support.

scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-camera appearance variation, detector noise, and distractor identities
evaluation_protocol_basis: Standard query-gallery retrieval protocol with Rank-1/mAP and identity labels
deployment_readiness_basis: Multi-camera surveillance imagery but retrieval-oriented
main_limitation: Query-gallery retrieval does not evaluate continuous trajectory tracking
pspr_interpretation: Useful for identity-discriminative appearance learning, but Re-ID accuracy alone does not guarantee online tracking.

scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-view identity matching with labelled and detector-generated boxes
evaluation_protocol_basis: CMC/mAP-style Re-ID protocol with identity labels
deployment_readiness_basis: Multi-camera campus surveillance but limited modern deployment variation
main_limitation: Does not evaluate online association or long-term identity persistence
pspr_interpretation: Supports Re-ID representation learning, not end-to-end MPT evaluation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Occlusion, cross-camera matching, distractors, and real surveillance scenes
evaluation_protocol_basis: Standard Re-ID query-gallery protocol with Rank-1/mAP
deployment_readiness_basis: Campus surveillance context but limited current accessibility
main_limitation: Not a full online tracking benchmark; access and usage restrictions may apply
pspr_interpretation: Historically important Re-ID resource, but current use requires careful access/governance checks.

scenario_complexity_basis: Illumination variation, camera diversity, time-of-day variation, and background changes
evaluation_protocol_basis: Large-scale Re-ID query-gallery protocol with identity-sensitive retrieval metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Indoor/outdoor multi-camera scenes with stronger visual diversity
main_limitation: Retrieval-oriented and does not directly evaluate detection-to-track association
pspr_interpretation: Useful for robust appearance embedding learning under diverse camera and illumination settings.

primary_role_label: Video-based person Re-ID and temporal appearance modelling
scenario_complexity_basis: Tracklet-level identity matching with detector/tracker noise and temporal appearance changes
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracklet retrieval protocol with identity-sensitive Rank/mAP metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Video tracklets from multi-camera surveillance, but not online tracking
main_limitation: Tracklet retrieval differs from complete MOT evaluation
pspr_interpretation: Useful for temporal Re-ID features and tracklet matching, but not for full detector-association evaluation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Occlusion, clutter, lighting variation, and viewpoint change across image sequences
evaluation_protocol_basis: Sequence-level retrieval protocol with CMC/rank-based Re-ID metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Public surveillance sequences but limited scale
main_limitation: Limited number of identities and cameras restricts large-scale tracking generalization
pspr_interpretation: Useful for sequence-based Re-ID evaluation under occlusion and viewpoint change.

scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-view illumination, pose, and viewpoint variation in a small setting
evaluation_protocol_basis: Rank/CMC-style Re-ID protocol
deployment_readiness_basis: Two-camera surveillance with limited scale and diversity
main_limitation: Small and less challenging than modern large-scale Re-ID datasets
pspr_interpretation: Useful as a legacy Re-ID reference, but limited for modern MPT benchmarking.

scenario_complexity_basis: Coupled pedestrian detection, full-scene search, and detector-dependent identity retrieval
evaluation_protocol_basis: Person-search mAP/top-k protocol linking detection and identity retrieval
deployment_readiness_basis: Full-scene multi-camera surveillance and detector-aware identity retrieval
main_limitation: Person-search benchmark rather than a full trajectory-level MOT benchmark
pspr_interpretation: Bridges detection and Re-ID, useful for detector-aware identity retrieval.

scenario_complexity_basis: Scene diversity, detection difficulty, distractors, and identity retrieval in full images
evaluation_protocol_basis: Person-search mAP/top-k protocol with detection plus retrieval
deployment_readiness_basis: Large-scale full-scene person search with deployment-relevant retrieval structure
main_limitation: Does not directly evaluate online temporal tracking or trajectory continuity
pspr_interpretation: Useful for person-search pipelines, but not a replacement for trajectory-level MPT benchmarks.

scenario_complexity_basis: Multi-person tracking, occlusion, crowd interaction, and surveillance-camera viewpoints
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking metrics and benchmark tasks, but less standardized than modern MOTChallenge-style protocols
deployment_readiness_basis: Real surveillance scenes but limited scale and scenario diversity
main_limitation: Limited in scale, diversity, and modern annotation richness
pspr_interpretation: Useful as an early surveillance tracking benchmark but limited for modern large-scale comparison.

scenario_complexity_basis: Pedestrian localization and short-term identity maintenance across views
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking protocol available, but limited modern benchmark support
deployment_readiness_basis: Multi-view pedestrian scenes with limited dataset scale
main_limitation: Small and less representative of modern large-scale MOT settings
pspr_interpretation: Useful for early multi-view pedestrian tracking, but not a modern comprehensive benchmark.

scenario_complexity_basis: Moving-camera tracking, scale variation, road-user interactions, and ego-motion
evaluation_protocol_basis: Official benchmark protocol, task-appropriate metrics, and public evaluation support
deployment_readiness_basis: Real autonomous-driving scenes with moving-camera relevance
main_limitation: Not primarily a dense multi-person pedestrian tracking benchmark
pspr_interpretation: Appropriate for driving-scene tracking and ego-motion contexts rather than dense surveillance MPT.

scenario_complexity_basis: Multi-object tracking under event-focused surveillance scenarios
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking metrics/protocols available but less central in modern MPT leaderboard comparison
deployment_readiness_basis: Real surveillance/event video with limited scale
main_limitation: Limited scale compared with recent large benchmarks
pspr_interpretation: Relevant to event-oriented surveillance tracking, but limited compared with modern MOT datasets.

scenario_complexity_basis: Detection quality, identity switches, occlusion, and short-term pedestrian association
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOTChallenge-style protocol with MOTA, IDF1, ID switches, and leaderboard support
deployment_readiness_basis: Mixed pedestrian tracking scenes but smaller than later MOTChallenge releases
main_limitation: Smaller and less standardized than later MOTChallenge datasets
pspr_interpretation: Useful as an early MOT benchmark; later MOT16/MOT17 are stronger for current comparison.

scenario_complexity_basis: Detection quality, occlusion handling, and identity association
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOTChallenge protocol with identity-sensitive metrics and leaderboard support
deployment_readiness_basis: Urban pedestrian tracking scenes with public/private detection relevance
main_limitation: Less diverse than later or more specialized datasets
pspr_interpretation: Standard pedestrian MOT benchmark for controlled comparison of tracking methods.

scenario_complexity_basis: Detection quality, moderate occlusion, identity switches, and pedestrian association
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOTChallenge public/private detection protocols, MOTA/IDF1/HOTA-style metrics, and leaderboard support
deployment_readiness_basis: Urban pedestrian tracking with standardized evaluation
main_limitation: Limited long-term re-entry and environmental diversity
pspr_interpretation: Core single-camera pedestrian MOT benchmark for public/private detection protocol interpretation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-camera identity association, occlusion, re-entry, and long-range multi-camera tracking
evaluation_protocol_basis: Multi-camera tracking/identity metrics and protocols; current access restrictions affect use
deployment_readiness_basis: Campus surveillance network with cross-camera structure
main_limitation: Access and usage constraints limit current benchmark availability
pspr_interpretation: Important historical multi-camera benchmark, but current use requires governance and access caution.

primary_role_label: Trajectory annotation and pedestrian tracking support
scenario_complexity_basis: Longer trajectory annotation, path supervision, and pedestrian motion continuity
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking-style protocol information, but less established as a central modern leaderboard
deployment_readiness_basis: Pedestrian video tracking scenes with longer path-oriented structure
main_limitation: Less commonly used as a central benchmark for modern MPT comparison
pspr_interpretation: Relevant for trajectory/path continuity studies, but not a dominant standard benchmark.

scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-camera calibration, multi-view localization, identity consistency, and crowd interactions
evaluation_protocol_basis: Multi-camera detection/localization/tracking protocol with calibrated views
deployment_readiness_basis: Multi-view surveillance with synchronized cameras
main_limitation: Limited scene scale and environmental variation
pspr_interpretation: Useful for calibrated multi-camera pedestrian localization and cross-view identity consistency.

primary_role_label: Multi-camera tracking in urban traffic settings
scenario_complexity_basis: Cross-camera association, re-entry, viewpoint change, and traffic-camera variation
evaluation_protocol_basis: Official challenge-style evaluation with multi-camera tracking/retrieval protocols
deployment_readiness_basis: Urban traffic camera network with deployment-relevant cross-camera re-entry
main_limitation: Primarily traffic-oriented with limited person-centric tracking focus
pspr_interpretation: Useful for cross-camera traffic tracking and re-entry evaluation, but not primarily pedestrian MPT.

scenario_complexity_basis: Vehicle detection, tracking, illumination variation, weather/traffic density, and surveillance viewpoints
evaluation_protocol_basis: Detection and tracking metrics with official benchmark-style evaluation
deployment_readiness_basis: Real traffic surveillance videos with diverse scene conditions
main_limitation: Vehicle-focused rather than person-centric
pspr_interpretation: Useful for traffic tracking and detection/tracking protocol analysis, not a pedestrian MPT benchmark.

scenario_complexity_basis: Joint object segmentation, tracking, identity preservation, and driving-scene camera motion
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOTS and tracking/segmentation metrics with benchmark protocols
deployment_readiness_basis: Driving-scene segmentation tracking with richer pixel-level annotation
main_limitation: Focused on cars and pedestrians in driving scenes rather than dense multi-person tracking
pspr_interpretation: Useful when segmentation-aware tracking is required, especially in driving-scene evaluation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Severe occlusion, high density, overlapping pedestrians, and identity switches under crowding
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOTChallenge public/private detection protocols and MOTA/IDF1/HOTA-style metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Dense pedestrian crowd scenes with strong crowding realism
main_limitation: Mainly focuses on dense crowds with limited scenario diversity
pspr_interpretation: Best suited for evaluating dense-crowd pedestrian tracking and occlusion-induced association failures.

scenario_complexity_basis: Long-term tracking, category diversity, target disappearance/reappearance, and open-world conditions
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking protocol with long-tail/open-world benchmark support
deployment_readiness_basis: Diverse long videos with strong open-world and deployment-like variation
main_limitation: Less specialized for dense multi-person pedestrian tracking
pspr_interpretation: Strong resource for long-term/open-world tracking, re-entry, and long-tail evaluation.

scenario_complexity_basis: Moving camera, weather, illumination, road-scene diversity, and traffic-object variation
evaluation_protocol_basis: Task-specific detection/tracking protocols with benchmark support
deployment_readiness_basis: Large-scale driving scenes with diverse weather, time, and road conditions
main_limitation: Person tracking is only one component of a broader traffic-object setting
pspr_interpretation: Strong deployment-oriented driving benchmark, but not exclusively person-centric.

scenario_complexity_basis: Head-level tracking under extreme crowd density, occlusion, and small-object conditions
evaluation_protocol_basis: Tracking protocol with head-level localization/identity evaluation
deployment_readiness_basis: Crowded pedestrian scenes with strong crowd-monitoring relevance
main_limitation: Does not provide complete full-body person trajectories
pspr_interpretation: Useful for extreme crowd head tracking, but not a full-body pedestrian MPT replacement.

scenario_complexity_basis: Controlled variation in viewpoint, density, lighting, segmentation, and identity
evaluation_protocol_basis: Detection/tracking/segmentation annotations with synthetic benchmark protocols
deployment_readiness_basis: Scalable synthetic urban scenes with controlled variation; real-world transfer must be verified
main_limitation: Synthetic-to-real domain gap may affect real-world generalization
pspr_interpretation: Useful for scalable training and controlled annotation, but should be validated on real datasets.

scenario_complexity_basis: Similar appearance, nonlinear motion, frequent interaction, and weak appearance cues
evaluation_protocol_basis: HOTA, DetA, AssA, MOTA, IDF1, and association-sensitive tracking metrics
deployment_readiness_basis: Motion-heavy videos useful for stressing association robustness
main_limitation: Less representative of conventional surveillance or traffic deployment
pspr_interpretation: Best suited for testing identity association under similar appearance and complex motion.

scenario_complexity_basis: Fast motion, similar uniforms, camera panning, and frequent interactions
evaluation_protocol_basis: MOT metrics such as HOTA, MOTA, IDF1 with benchmark-style evaluation
deployment_readiness_basis: Team-sports video with strong motion and interaction realism
main_limitation: Domain-specific and may not generalize to surveillance or traffic scenes
pspr_interpretation: Useful for sports analytics and motion-heavy association benchmarking.
